Project Name: Case Management Solution (CMS)
Project Description: Establishing a fit-for-purpose case management system for the Administrative Review Tribunal (ART).

Project Name: Big Data Timely Insights (BDTI) Phase 2
Project Description: Designed as the pathway off legacy ICT systems ensuring the ABS maximises the reuse of new tools and lessons across the organisation. The project involves rebuilding and securing the Consumer Price Index (CPI) ICT system, building the business statistics production process in the cloud, rebuilding the Business Characteristics Asset ICT system, and industrialising the ABS cloud environment.

Project Name: Working Smarter Program (WSP)
Project Description: The investment will stabilise the ACCC’s ICT infrastructure to support the installation of fit-for-purpose business and corporate enabling applications and systems and introduce data analytics capabilities.

Project Name:
National Anti-Scam
Centre (NASC) ICT Program
Project Description: The NASC is a world-leading initiative to make Australia the hardest country for scammers to victimise citizens in. It will disincentivise scammers from targeting Australia and result in significantly less scam attempts.

Project Name: National Criminal Intelligence System (NCIS)
Project Description: The NCIS will provide secure access to a national view of criminal information and intelligence.

Project Name:
National Automated Fingerprint
Identification System (NAFIS) NextGen
Project Description: The NAFIS NextGen will provide improved system serviceability by implementing a modern, future-proof software platform and cloud infrastructure hosting.

Project Name: Spectrum Management
Project Description: The new digital spectrum management system will include a range of new and enhanced digital and ICT capabilities to support improved management of spectrum.

Project Name: Fighting Scams - SMS Sender ID registry
Project Description: This project is enabling ACMA to develop, operate and maintain an SMS ID registry. This will assist in preventing scammers from spoofing industry or government brand names (such as CBA or myGov) in message headers.

Project Name: National Climate Risk Assessment
Project Description: The National Climate Risk Assessment is intended to identify and analyse nationally significant climate-related risks for Australia for action by the National Adaptation Plan. These are risks whose consequences would be pervasive and prolonged, and thus will require a national coordinated response. A DCA assessment is currently in progress and expected to be available on 23 February.

Project Name:
My Health Record on Fast Health
Interoperability Resources
Project Description: This project is establishing a new National My Health Record (MHR) Repository Service that will improve the capabilities of the MHR system. This involves replacing the existing legacy MHR repository with a modernised, scalable service that is aligned with contemporary, international health industry data standards, in particular Health Level 7 (HL7) Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR).

Project Name:
Indigo - Election Systems
Modernisation Program
Project Description: The Indigo Program aims to modernise the AEC’s core ICT infrastructure and systems to deliver an integrated, secure, modern electoral system that meets community needs and expectations into the future. The replacement of ageing legacy systems with modernised electoral management systems and infrastructure will improve the AEC’s ability to manage security risks and strengthen Australia’s ongoing democratic resilience.

Project Name: Polling Place Technology Project
Project Description: Enhanced technology to enable expansion of voter & polling coverage of polling place technology (up to 10,000 ECL devices) - Electronic Certified Lists (ECLs) are portable computers deployed to polling places as a replacement to paper certified lists. The program is trialling a digital officer in charge return at selected polling locations

Project Name: Australian Energy Regulator - Data and Digital Transformation
Project Description: The AER is planning to transform the agency's data and digital systems through the AER Digital Transformation project. This project will position the AER to improve data driven policy and decisions that support all energy market stakeholders and consumers. Accordingly, it will align with AER’s vision to deliver better outcomes for consumers, stakeholders, and staff by transforming AER’s digital experience with technologies that connect, integrate, collaborate, and operate seamlessly.

Project Name: Investigation Management Solution (IMS) Program
Project Description: The Investigations Management Solution (IMS) provides a single software platform for AFP members to manage investigations throughout their lifecycle, from a report being received through to brief generation and finalization.

Project Name: Establishment of the National Anti- Corruption Commission (NACC)
Project Description: In establishing the independent National Anti Corruption Commission, as part of the overall funding provided by Government in the 2022-23 October Budget, $63.5 million was provided for the establishment of an 'Independent ICT environment for the Commission.’

Project Name: Ensuring Radiation Protection and Nuclear Safety for All Australians
Project Description: ARPANSA is presently developing a prototype of a self-service portal that will enable customers of the agency’s Personal Radiation Monitoring Service (PRMS) to access a range of online capabilities. This project has been prompted by both internal and external stakeholders who have identified an urgent need to improve the current processes, enhance accessibility, and fulfil customer expectations.

Project Name:
Fighting
Investment Scams
Project Description: The Fighting Investment Scams project aims to protect Australian consumers from investment scams by proactively taking down investment scam and phishing websites. The project covers the ongoing use of a takedown service to remove a variety of scam investment websites (including scam crypto-asset investment websites) and phishing websites.

Project Name:
Modernising ATO IT Systems (MATOS)
Program
Project Description: The Modernising ATO IT Systems (MATOS) Program first tranche, Insights and Intelligence, ($42.2 million partial funding) will modernise the ATO’s strategic IT application platform as the first step to unlock the full data potential of the ATO by purposefully leveraging data, analytics and automation to maximise the long-term sustainable economic value to Australia from the tax and superannuation system.

Project Name: Payday Super - Superannuation Guarantee Reform (Unpaid Super)
Project Description:
The Payday Super - Superannuation Guarantee (SG) reform project will address the systemic issue of non- and under-payment of SG entitlements by employers by moving the obligation to pay from a quarterly to payday cycle.
The ATO was funded to: (1) create a data foundation for the ATO to engage employers at scale to encourage them to review and self-correct where they may not have met their SG obligations and (2) undertake consultation and co-design to develop a second pass business case outlining the changes and implications required to support an employer payday SG obligation.

Project Name:
Implementation of GloBE
and Domestic Minimum Tax
Project Description: This project is providing the ATO with the means to ensure resources are available for the ICT and data requirements to administer the OECD measure entitled: Global Anti-Base Erosion Model Rules (Pillar Two) as well as the complementing Domestic Minimum Tax (DMT).

Project Name: Better Targeted Superannuation Concessions (formerly Large Balance Cap - $3m (Superannuation))
Project Description: The Better Targeted Superannuation Concessions measure was announced by the Government on 28 February 2023, with further authority and detail provided in the 2023-24 Budget. This project is enabling the delivery of this measure with first assessments to be issued in the first half of the 2026-27 financial year.

Project Name:
Cyber Security Program (previously
Building Cyber Resilience)
Project Description: The Program focuses on uplifting Essential 8 to Maturity Level 2, providing fit for purpose security services to protect the ATO’s digital ecosystem. The Program’s outcomes will increase the ATO’s Cyber maturity through the delivery of enhanced cyber capabilities and technologies.

Project Name:
Contemporary Excise
Experience (CEE) Project
Project Description: The Contemporary Excise Experience has improved and streamlined processing obligations for excise clients and staff so the ATO can provide a consistent experience and continue to enhance the tailored service and relationships these clients value. The project has enabled electronic lodgement, online view and processing of returns and claims.

Project Name: Case Management Project (CMP)
Project Description:
The Case Management project is developing an enterprise wide case management and customer relationship management system to:
-      support enhanced regulatory, enforcement and intelligence capabilities,
-      provide an agency wide accessible visibility of entities that AUSTRAC interacts with, and
-      support referrals / document sharing between AUSTRAC teams and to partner agencies as well as provide coordination of evidence management functions.
The system will provide an end to end capability covering day to day interactions with reporting entities, partner agencies and industry participants.

Project Name:
Reporting Entity System
Transformation (REST)
Project Description:
The REST Program is implementing a new, secure, industry facing reporting system that will better support AUSTRAC's regulatory and intelligence functions, streamline communication with industry, and make it easier for industry, particularly small businesses, to comply with reporting obligations. The program will:
-      address existing system limitations that impede compliance by AUSTRAC reporting entities
-      overcome system instabilities that cause regular, intermittent system failures, and
-      manage increasing volumes of financial data from the financial sector to AUSTRAC.

Project Name:
ROBUST
Program
Project Description: The ROBUST program is securing and strengthening all elements of the Bureau's operating environment to provide continuous availability of critical services and mitigate risks arising from the fragility of the existing technology environment.

Project Name: Water Market Reform - Strengthening Integrity and Transparency
Project Description:
This project is restoring transparency, integrity and confidence to water markets through a new single digital platform for national water data management, a new water market website and water market data standards.
Budget of $33m over 4 years then moving to $3m p.a. ongoing Build component ends 30 June 2027

Project Name:
Evidence Management Modernisation (formerly Digital
Litigation Solution)
Project Description: This investment is modernising how digital evidentiary material is managed by The Office of the Commonwealth Director of Public Prosecutions by introducing an independent system (software and hardware) with the technical capability—enabled by machine learning and artificial intelligence—to effectively manage increasingly large volumes of digital evidence. This involves the seamless transition from legacy systems and establishment of optimum support arrangements to ensure the ongoing effective and efficient delivery of prosecution services.

Project Name: The Clean Energy Regulator (CER) Change Program
Project Description: This program is responsible for delivering new business and technological capability to support commercial drivers for emissions reduction across nine projects. The intended outcome of these projects is to “Streamline private sector action to support emissions reductions.

Project Name: Enterprise Client and Workload Management (ECWM)
Project Description: This project is implementing an enterprise ICT capability for a Client and Workload Management solution that is scalable and reusable. The objectives include making it easier for businesses and individuals to deal with the department through the delivery of streamlined business processes and improved access to digital services. They also include supporting a cost effective, modern and client focused service delivery model with increasing client access to online services, facilitating ease of compliance with regulatory requirements and improving the effectiveness of biosecurity risk management. Finally, the project will elevate workload management to a national level, to optimise the use of resources and ensure the right people with the right competencies are available to complete work.

Project Name: Busting Congestion for Agriculture Exporters (Take Farmers to Markets)
Project Description: The Take Farmers to Markets (TFTM) program is transforming Australia's agricultural export systems with the delivery of contemporary digital products and services for trade and exporter engagement. Tranche 1 established the foundations for the digital reforms by uplifting existing systems onto modern and reliable platforms and developing new digital services. Tranche 2 builds upon the capabilities delivered in Tranche 1 through delivery of a dynamic program of work that enables the department to become a risk-based, data-driven, digital regulator.

Project Name: Simplified Targeting and Enhanced Processing Systems (STEPS)
Project Description: The Simplified Trade and Enhanced Processing System (STEPS) program will transform the border process for cargo importers who depend on the Department of Agriculture to mitigate and regulate Biosecurity risk in a challenging global marketplace.

Project Name: Supporting a Stronger and More Sustainable Agriculture Sector
Project Description: This proposal seeks to uplift the ICT, data and statistical systems capabilities to meet the needs of department. This will improve the accessibility of agricultural data and information, improve and remediate survey processing systems, rebuild geospatial infrastructure and gather requirements on future capabilities necessary to transform and enable agricultural data and information products to suit changing needs.

Project Name: Improve Energy Planning
Project Description: This measure seeks to improve energy planning and inform investment in energy networks and enabling infrastructure, including by developing  new or improved energy forecasting models that enable more granular and integrated analysis of future energy demand.

Project Name: Establishing a National Environment Protection Agency
Project Description: The aim of this project is to establish a new independent Commonwealth agency, known as Environment Protection Australia, to enforce national environmental laws and restore confidence in Australia’s environmental protection system. The funding usage includes both operating costs and one-off transitional costs.

Project Name: Commonwealth Climate Risk and Opportunity Management Program
Project Description: To enhance climate risk management capabilities across the Commonwealth public sector, a new climate risk management guide and learning and development tools have been developed. To support this capability uplift the climate risk management digital tool is currently being developed. This will be a user-friendly digital tool that provides the necessary data, knowledge and guidance to Australian Public Service agencies to produce climate risk assessment reports.

Project Name: Engaging with First Nations Peoples on Climate Change
Project Description: The Government has committed $15.9 million to engage with First Nations people on climate adaptation, including the establishment of a centre focused on the Torres Strait and Northern Peninsula Area. The centre is being co-designed with senior leaders and the Torres Strait Regional Authority, and will enable a coordinated regional response to climate change on sea level rise and infrastructure, water and food security, sustaining and strengthening culture, economic stability, access to renewables, and other opportunities. Three regional roundtables have been held with senior leaders in 2023, and in September 2023, senior leaders in the region agreed to high level principles for implementation of the Centre.

Project Name: Sustaining Environmental Assessments
Project Description: The Sustaining Environmental Assessments measure provided funds to extend this workflow system to include a searchable public register of Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act offsets (the National Environmental Offset System) and for integration with the compliance investigations ICT system. This work includes design of functional interfaces with the systems being developed for compliance purposes and risk rating so that these systems can operate in the future as modules of a more comprehensive approach to managing information relevant to the protection of the environment.

Project Name: Online Data Collection and Reporting Mechanism for the DEF Market
Project Description: The purpose of the market transparency measure for the Diesel Exhaust Fluid (DEF) sector is to establish a data collection and reporting framework for the DEF market to increase transparency and assist in avoiding future supply shortages.

Project Name: National EV public charging infrastructure roadmap
Project Description: Development of a Roadmap for zoning optimal locations for the development of fast and ultra-fast EV public charging infrastructure across Australia (report and online mapping tool). A cloud consulting services / platform as a service contract has been executed with a third party - with the consulting and build period from January to May 2024, the mapping tool to be publicly available by June/July 2024, and support and maintenance until May 2025.

Project Name: Housing Energy Upgrades Fund
Project Description: This proposal includes the discovery and alpha stages of a new Greenhouse and Energy Minimum Standards (GEMS) registration system. The current GEMS registration system is ageing and unable to support implementing the requirements of an updated GEMS Act.

Project Name:
Vocational Education and Training (VET) Student
Loans IT System (VSL) - Phase 1
Project Description: This project is enabling the Government to better realise the benefits of the VSL program and minimise the risk of occurrences such as the recent historical student debt issue. It will have a stronger focus on compliance and automation, and deliver a system that is a flexible, scalable and stable with a greatly enhanced data collection, integration and analytics capability.

Project Name: Apprentice Data Management System (ADMS)
Project Description: The purpose of ADMS is to build a new integrated system which is fit for purpose to better support apprentices, employers, delivery partners and the Government to help more people access and complete apprenticeships promoting positive contributions to Australia's post- COVID economic recovery. This will be achieved through building a new, modern, secure and stable system which enables system interface connections between government and delivery partners, a new dynamic employer and apprentice user interface enabling a personalised experience and improving data accessibility and connectivity.

Project Name: New Energy Apprenticeships
Project Description: New Energy Apprenticeships is an augmentation of the digital/ICT capability being established through ADMS to support delivery of the New Energy Apprenticeships Program.

Project Name: Onboarding complementary programs onto the Workforce Australia Digital Platform
Project Description: This project seeks to onboard three complementary programs from their existing legacy ICT systems to the Workforce Australia Digital Platform (the platform). Onboarding these complementary programs to the platform will deliver improvements to user experiences, program uptake, policy agility and better technical sustainability. Ultimately more individuals will be supported through these complementary programs to find work or start their own business.

Project Name: Targeted Support for Apprenticeships (Option 1)
Project Description: This project will introduce a new non-financial support model for Australian Apprenticeships from 1 July 2024. The model will redesign and refocus key support services currently delivered by the Australian Apprenticeship Support Network to increase apprenticeship completion rates and the diversity of the apprentice workforce.

Project Name: Foundation skills program redesign
Project Description: This project is providing approximately 27,000 Australians per year with access to language, literacy, numeracy, and digital  training. This will improve the employability of Australians, improving the prospects of Australians who struggle with no employment, or low-pay unsecure employment.

Project Name: Pacific Australia Labour Mobility (PALM) scheme insourcing
Project Description: The project is enabling transfer of the Pacific Australia Labour Mobility Information System (PALMIS) to the department’s infrastructure, between July 2023 and December 2023, followed by PALMIS Expansion and Enhancement and Telephony Integration. When completed, PALMIS will provide a robust and future-proof solution for ongoing management of  the program including enabling strong oversight and reporting functionality. This is essential given the high-profile nature of the scheme, the Government’s plan to Build a Stronger Pacific Family, the  vulnerability of workers and the importance of the scheme to industry to assist in meeting unmet labour needs in regional and rural Australia (and agriculture nationally).

Project Name: Time to Work Employment Service - Extension and Replacement
Project Description: This project is supporting First Nations prisoners to connect with employment services and to help more First Nations prisoners move from prison to paid work. This includes extending the Time to Work Employment Service (TWES) in non-remote areas for 12 months to 30 June 2024, with a new servicing model being introduced from 1 July 2024. As part of the project the department is also seeking changes to the Capability Building Fund (that supports new employment service providers) in order to support TWES providers to fulfil the department’s requirements for accreditation in the department’s ICT cybersecurity protection arrangements, so that they can access the Workforce Australia Digital Program (WADP).

Project Name:
Foreign Arrangements Scheme (FAS): Ensuring Australian foreign engagement
in the national interest
Project Description: This project will ensure the ongoing enhancement and maintenance of the Foreign Arrangements Scheme Online Portal and Website, including the Public Register. This project will also ensure the Government is able to meet its legislative responsibilities under the Australia’s Foreign Relations (State and Territory Arrangements) Act 2020 and be responsive to stakeholder feedback.

Project Name:
Keeping the lights on -
International Communications Network (ICN) Stabilisation
Project Description:
This project will establish an immediate program of work to stabilise the International Communications Network (ICN) by replacing existing assets and a refresh of core ICT infrastructure components (servers/network).
Performance stabilisation of the ICN is critical to improving the ICN’s cyber security resilience, network reliability, and functionality in the short- and medium-term.

Project Name: Single Business Service
Project Description:
This project will maintain and review the existing Single Business Service (business.gov.au and contact centre) to simplify small to medium businesses engagement with government.
Note: the budget figure includes $16.4 million in ICT.

Project Name:
Securing Questacon's Critical and Core Operations and Safety - Questacon
Digital Futures Project
Project Description: Currently, Questacon’s software, hardware and network infrastructure do not provide a consistent, accessible customer experience. Current systems also do not meet core requirements for seamless digital transactions for users, efficient operations and secure transactions. This project is addressing these issues through targeted investment and uplift in key areas.

Project Name: An Enduring Commonwealth Function for Radioactive Waste Management
Project Description: This project represents the intention of the Commonwealth to ensure safe and secure management of Australia's radioactive waste to protect people and the environment for generations to come. The project seeks to fund the Australian Radioactive Waste Agency (ARWA) to deliver an enduring function for radioactive waste management in Australia.

Project Name: National Reconstruction Fund (NRF)
Project Description: "The project will build on the Government’s commitment to establish the NRF Corporation (NRFC) to rebuild Australia’s industrial base. The Corporation will provide a crucial financing vehicle to drive investment and provide financial assistance (including loans and guarantees) and equity investments to support projects that create secure well-paid jobs, diversify, and transform Australia’s industry and economy in priority areas.

Project Name: Reporting and Program Management (RPM)
Project Description:
The Infrastructure Investment Transformation Program (IITP) is delivering improvements to administration of grant payments to states and  territories for infrastructure projects.  Following recommendations from the Australian National Audit Office, the Department is developing a new system which has been designed to address evolving business needs and transition the current legacy system to a more scalable and flexible system. The RPM Project Objectives are: (1) Develop a new system to automate, simplify and streamline business processes. (2) Provide a single source of data for all grant process stages that is validated and accurate, easy to access and analyse. (3) Ensure the new system is intuitive, easy to use and flexible, and can be extended to meet future needs. (4) Migrate data from the existing data sources into a  consolidated source of truth for infrastructure investment.
Note: the budget figure does not include approximately $11m on RPM development from 2019-2022. The total budget includes sustainment funding until 30 June 2028.

Project Name:
Next Phase
of the National Disability Data Asset (NDDA)
Project Description: The project will establish an enduring National Disability Data Asset (NDDA) of linked, de-identified data, leveraging multiple Australian, state and territory government service systems and surveys to provide insights on the outcomes of people with disability and their pathways through services.

Project Name:
Modernisation to reduce wait
times to access support and services
Project Description: This proposal seeks to ensure the Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA's) technology and digital channel delivery functions effectively to support improved claims administration and claims backlog elimination; and to address the unacceptable level of enterprise risk associated with the legacy technology that underpins DVA's financial management capability.

Project Name:
Replacement of the
Departmental On Line Accounting and Reporting System (DOLARS),
previously known as Modernisation to Reduce Wait
Time to Access Support and Services (2PBC) (DOLARS)
Project Description: This project directly addresses key issues underpinning the Royal Commission’s recommendations by proposing a range of measures to address the unacceptable level of enterprise risk associated with the legacy technology underpinning DVA’s financial management capability (DOLARS) and ensure DVA’s technology and digital channel delivery functions support improve claims administration and claims backlog elimination.

Project Name:
Tertiary
Collection of Student
Information (TCSI) –
Remediation and analytic renewal
Project Description: Deliver the functionality necessary for the TCSI platform to clear the existing student loan backlog, ensure the security and privacy of current data holdings, ensure emerging data collection needs are error free and enable robust reporting on Commonwealth support schemes across Government. Department of Education has internally treated Remediation (communicating with students and education providers, and correcting data including loan balances to ensure no student is unduly financially disadvantaged) as a separate and distinct phase from Renewal (reducing risk of future data issues). Both phases are effectively in flight (Remediation in transition to BAU, Renewal project live) as at January 2024. The project end date for the renewal phase is 30 June 2026.

Project Name:
Transforming program
administration for schools and higher education
Project Description: The scope is to address the risks through the modernisation of the technology systems and processes involved in the collection of education provider data for entitlement management, assessment and calculation  of eligibility and entitlements disbursement of payments to schools and higher education providers, compliance and acquittal requirements, and reporting and data analysis needs.

Project Name: School Unique Student Identifier (USI)
Project Description: The project is focused on implementing the foundation pieces and mechanisms to enable the assignment of school student USIs (Unique Student Identifiers).

Project Name: Plan for Cheaper Child Care
Project Description: New data infrastructure to replace the existing child care data warehouse, analytics and reporting processes with a robust end-to-end solution.

Project Name: Digital ID Program
Project Description: The Australian Government is investing in the transformation of its customer experience to ensure government services are simple, helpful, respectful, transparent, and structured around people’s needs. The Australian Government Digital ID System (AGDIS) is a critical piece of this national digital infrastructure, and a fundamental enabler of both government service delivery and the broader digital economy. It will make it faster for people to prove who they are and make it easier to access services online.

Project Name: Parliamentary Expenses Management System
Project Description: The Parliamentary Expenses Management System (PEMS) is automating claim processing and providing integrated online services to provide up to date information and efficiently support parliamentarians in their official duties. While expense management is the focus, PEMS includes a human resources and payroll system for MOP(S) Act staff, a financial management system and reporting for the 227 Parliamentarians offices, increasing transparency and accountability. The eighth and final module of PEMS (Expenditure Reporting) was successfully delivered on 22 November 2023 and IPEA recommenced public reporting on 20 December 2023.

Project Name: Improving transparency of procurement data to support the Buy Australian Plan
Project Description: This initiative supports the implementation of the Buy Australian Plan by improving the transparency of Australian Government procurement activity through enhanced AusTender capabilities including  the establishment of a dedicated and integrated panel supplier portal; and improving AusTender reporting capability to increase transparency.

Project Name: Provider Operations and Dollars Going to Care ICT Solution (Provider Operations)
Project Description: The project is to deliver an ICT solution to support collection, collation and transformation of information about an approved providers' operations (including income, expenditure on care, nursing, food, maintenance, cleaning, administration and profit and loss) and for making the information publicly available on the My Aged Care website.

Project Name:
Essential aged care
information and communication technologies system maintenance and enhancements (Future Aged Care ICT
Platform)
Project Description: The primary goals for this investment are to support reforms to the Aged Care system by delivering ICT solutions that reduce provider administration burden, improve the quality and transparency of key data across the and reduce ongoing ICT costs across Health and Aged Care as systems are standardised and consolidated on a modern ICT platform.

Project Name: Support at Home
Project Description: The program aims to provide consumers with a single system for support at home that puts them at the centre of their care. It seeks to ensure that consumers receive funding and services that match their needs and supports them to remain independent at home.

Project Name:
Aged Care
ICT to Enable Reform: new Aged Care Act
Project Description: This project is upgrading Aged Care ICT capability as an essential enabler of the new Aged Care Act (‘the new Act’) through a progressively staged ICT investment program to operationalise Aged Care reform.

Project Name:
Transition of the National COVID-19
Vaccine Program
- Vaccine Data and Digital
Project Description: COVID-19 Vaccinations data and digital project will sustain platforms through Winter 2023 and transition to sustainable, business as usual arrangements through 2023 and early 2024. The platforms are critical to ensuring people can access COVID vaccines at times and locations that meet their needs.

Project Name:
Registered nurses on site 24/7 and More carers with more time to care (Australian
National Aged Care
Classification (AN-ACC) Care
Minutes)
Project Description: This project supports implementation of the commitment to make nurses available at all residential aged care facilities, 24/7 from 1 July 2023 and to increase care minutes from 1 October 2024.

Project Name:
Essential aged care
information and communication technologies system maintenance and enhancements (ACFR/QFR)
Project Description: The primary goal for this investment is to redevelop the Quarterly Financial Report through which providers supply crucial financial and service quality information to government, ensuring that aged care reforms around transparency and accountability can be delivered.

Project Name:
Establish
the Inspector- General of Aged Care and the Office of the
Inspector- General of Aged Case
as a Statutory Agency
Project Description: This investment will enable the establishment of the Inspector-General and its supporting statutory office, which includes the development of ICT components to support ongoing standalone business operations. It includes a primary stakeholder management system build and an independent website for the Inspector-General.

Project Name:
Setting the AN- ACC price for 2023-24 and ongoing
residential care funding reform
Project Description:
This project will support implementation arrangements for the Government’s election commitment to fund the Fair Work Commission decision for residential care aged care workers, and the annual adjustment in Australian National Aged Care Classification (ANACC) funding to account for changes in aged care costs such as inflation.
Note: the budget only includes the ICT budget.

Project Name:
Establish a
National Worker Registration Scheme for Aged Care
Project Description: This project is enabling the Government’s commitment to establish a national worker registration scheme. The project is continuing development of an ICT solution for worker screening that will support policy implementation and develop English proficiency and ongoing training elements of the scheme.

Project Name:
Risk Based Targeting and Information
Sharing Phase 3 and Home Care Project (RBTIS)

Therapeutic Goods
Administration (aka HPRG Transformation Program)
Project Description: Modernising Health Products Regulation Group (HPRG) business systems, infrastructure and joining up services to reduce the administrative burden on Australian businesses, healthcare professionals and patients in their interactions with the Therapeutic Goods Administration.

Project Name:
Funding for the Regulation of Medicinal Cannabis - Cost Recovery Model Update and
Office of Drug Control Business Transformation
Project Description: This project supports the transformation of the Office of Drug Control business processes and ICT/digital systems. Prior reliance on outdated manual and inefficient business processes have impacted Australia’s legal narcotics industry and this project will implement the outcomes of a review undertaken in 2022 to modernise systems to ensure they can support the industry and compliance activity, as well as aligning with other regulators’ digital transformation systems.

Project Name:
Preparatory Work for
Implementation of New Legislation to Modernise the National Gene Technology Scheme - more
effective delivery of regulation
Project Description: This project seeks to undertake preparatory work to implement legislative change, with system enhancements to enable efficient and effective operation of the new legislation. A fully integrated portal system will also be built to support stakeholder engagement and compliance with the regulatory system, and increase transparency of the operation of the Gene Technology Scheme.

Project Name: Seamless Traveller (IDEMIA Life Extension project)
Project Description: The IDEMIA Life Extension project (the IDEMIA project) will deliver a supported automated border clearance capability that underpins critical business operations for the ABF. It is focused on sustainment of capability rather than transformation. It will replace ageing Kiosks and SmartGates within existing airport arrivals terminals across Australia with a modern Generation 3 (Gen3) alternative.

Project Name:
Visa
Modernisation/ Digitisation (Sustaining
Visa Processing Capability) (SVPC)
Project Description: The proposed investment will improve existing visa systems in Home Affairs. Enhancements will be designed for reuse and deliver broad operational benefits. This investment is not a large, transformational reform of the migration ICT system. It is an interim solution to enhance the existing ICT visa systems to improve visa service delivery to increase Australia’s attractiveness as a destination for travellers, temporary residents and migrants.

Project Name:
Paperless trading for Australian exporters - a model for
implementing a Digital
Verification Platform for
trade documents (DVP)
Project Description: Implementation of a Digital Verification Platform for priority trade documentation that will simplify trade for Australian exporters with trusted digital trade documents.

Project Name: Patents Admin Management System
Project Description: The project aims to modernise technologies and infrastructure that form part of the existing Rights In One platform. To further support the maintainability, security and scalability, the platform will be rehosted in a cloud environment and utilize some new technology opportunities that have matured since the conclusion of the Trade Marks and Designs project a few years ago.

Project Name: Future Proofing the Murray- Darling Basin
Project Description:
This project is implementing the Government’s commitment to safeguard and future-proof the Murray-Darling Basin (Basin) by preparing for, and undertaking in full, the statutory Basin Plan Review (the Review).
Outcomes include enabling data and ICT required to support the review, making data and information publicly available and accessible to a range of audiences, and to manage delivery of the work program.

Project Name: Cyber Upgrades to Reduce Exposure (CURE) Program
Project Description: The Cyber Upgrades to Reduce Exposure (CURE) program is designed to deliver a broad range of Cyber Security and ICT uplift initiatives for PM&C and its shared service clients. This multi-year program aims to strengthen PM&C's cyber security posture, uplift ICT capabilities and improve compliance against the relevant mandatory Whole of Australian Government ICT strategies and policies.

Project Name: Health Delivery Modernisation (HDM) Program (Phase 3 & 3A)
Project Description: This project is delivering new health transformation capabilities to better enable primary health care reform and strengthen service interoperability as well as deliver new digital health services that deliver the Government’s vision of placing the needs of customers and businesses first. It is also enabling implementation of Strengthening Medicare initiatives, particularly MyMedicare, as well as delivering immediate improvements by digitising further health services and better connecting health data for users and health professionals.

Project Name:
Government Enterprise Resource
Planning
Project Description:
The GovERP Programme was being delivered by Services Australia, in collaboration with the Department of Finance. On 28 November 2023, the Minister for Finance, Senator the Hon Katy Gallagher, announced significant changes to the Shared Services Transformation Program and the previously approved GovERP Programme, which resulted in a fundamental change to the delivery of the ERP solution. It was announced that agencies will no longer be mandated to utilise GovERP, leaving agencies free to explore appropriate solutions to best meet their needs and budget, with GovERP to be repurposed for use by the Services Australia and any other interested agencies. The Digital Transformation Agency (DTA) is undertaking an independent re-use assessment of the GovERP Programme, with future requirements to be considered by Government.
The GovERP Programme has been the subject of a second enhanced notification letter under the Enhanced Notification Process.

Project Name:
Energy Bill Relief Fund (EBRF) –
ICT Build for Services Australia
Project Description:
As part of the Energy Price Relief Plan, the Australian Government is partnering with states and territories to deliver targeted and temporary relief on power bills to eligible Australian households and small businesses that are customers of electricity retailers. Bill relief payments commenced on schedule from 1 July 2023.
Treasury is the policy owner and project lead. Services Australia was engaged in the project to provide Centrelink Confirmation eServices (CCeS), which is a secure online service for businesses to confirm a customer’s entitlement to a concession, rebate or service. A temporary solution in CCeS was created for this measure with the plan to decommission this functionality in June 2024, to support formal project closure. This workflow relates solely to collecting customer consent on the electricity retailer/state/territory’s behalf.
The DCA rating of “Medium” in December 2023 reflected the possibility, at that time, that decommissioning of the temporary CCeS workflow, and formal project closure, may occur after June 2024. The CCeS workflow decommissioning and project closure are currently on track for 30 June 2024 as planned. Retailers will still be able to use the CCeS system to confirm concession or payment entitlement for rebates after the workflow is closed. All other elements of this project have been delivered successfully and on schedule.

Project Name: Strengthening Medicare - Chronic Wound Consumables Scheme for Patients with Diabetes
Project Description: This project is improving the management of wounds for patients in primary care settings by providing education and training for healthcare professionals and providing fully subsidised wound consumable products to people with chronic wounds. The pilot phase of this project is limited to people with a chronic wound and diabetes who are aged 65 and over (or aged 50 and over for First Nations people). If the pilot is successful, the program may be expanded to all Australians with chronic wounds.

Project Name:
Foreign
Investment Digital Transformation (FIDT) Program
Project Description:
The Treasury is undertaking a transformation program to support the Government’s foreign investment framework and replace the current Foreign Investment Management System to deliver a fully functional end- to-end case management system, a register of foreign ownership of Australian assets and improved analytics capability.
The FIDT program has been the subject of a second enhanced notification letter under the Enhanced Notification Process.
